title: San Diego Mosque Shooting: What to Know About the Suspects and What Happened
description: Three men were killed in the attack at the Islamic Center of San Diego. Two teenage suspects were found dead in a car nearby, police said.
author: Claire Moses

title: Georgia Republicans are grasping for a contender to challenge Senator Jon Ossoff.
author: Tim Balk

title: The war on Iran has complicated a closely watched Republican primary.
author: Jennifer Medina

title: Here’s the latest.
author: Shane Goldmacher

title: G.O.P. Supporters Back Trump, but a Third Seek a New Direction for the Party
description: While President Trump remains overwhelmingly popular within the Republican coalition, a New York Times/Siena poll found, a sizable share wants the party’s next nominee to take a different approach.
author: Patricia Mazzei, Ruth Igielnik and Camille Baker

title: Can the Democrats Win Back Pennsylvania’s Working Class?
description: Republicans flipped Pennsylvania’s 7th Congressional District in 2024. Now, Democrats hope to win it back. Their strategy: appealing to voters on cost-of-living issues.
author: Sabrina Tavernise, Kassie Bracken, Mark Boyer and Emily Rhyne

title: What to Watch in Tuesday’s Elections in Kentucky, Georgia and Beyond
description: A prominent Republican critic of President Trump is trying to hang on in Kentucky, and other states are also holding primaries that will test Mr. Trump’s power over his party.
author: Reid J. Epstein

title: College Republicans Split Over Whether to Embrace the Far Right
description: As some college Republicans invite white nationalists into their organizations, other young conservatives have recoiled. The divide could affect upcoming elections.
author: Stephanie Saul

title: Motherhood Changed Ilana Glazer. A Reporter Knew What That Felt Like.
description: Sometimes a reporter and subject can converse on a common ground.
author: Remy Tumin

title: Last-Minute Donation Could Upend G.O.P. Primary for Texas Attorney General
description: A single donor’s contribution of $2.75 million could help propel Representative Chip Roy in his clash with a self-funded state senator, Mayes Middleton.
author: Lauren McGaughy

title: San Diego Mosque Shooting Comes Amid Rising Reports of Islamophobia in the U.S.
description: The Council on American-Islamic Relations said complaints about bias against Muslims are at levels unseen in decades.
author: Bernard Mokam

title: Trump Is Said to Have Proposed a New Helipad at the White House
description: The exhaust from helicopters’ engines has been known to occasionally scorch the grass on their landing zones.
author: Luke Broadwater and Minho Kim

title: A Fearful Phone Call and Frantic Search Preceded San Diego Mosque Shooting
description: San Diego police arrived to find three people dead. Nearby, they found two teenagers, the attackers, dead in a car. The violence shattered an idyllic Southern California city.
author: Tim Arango, Christina Morales, Candice Reed and Chelsia Rose Marcius

title: Sandy Fire in Simi Valley Threatens Thousands of Homes
description: Officials ordered more than 29,000 to evacuate after a brush fire broke out in Simi Valley, Calif.
author: Soumya Karlamangla and Amy Graff

title: Justices Hint at Strains as Supreme Court Comes Under Scrutiny
description: Traveling across the country, justices defend the role of the court even as strained relations among its members emerge in writing and remarks.
author: Ann E. Marimow and Aishvarya Kavi

title: Georgia Republicans Grasp for a Contender to Take On Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff
description: On the eve of their primary, Republicans have grown nervous about their prospects in November against Mr. Ossoff, regarded as the most vulnerable Democratic senator in the fall midterms.
author: Tim Balk

title: He’s Been Impeached and Indicted. He Has a Chance in Texas’ Senate Race.
description: The fate of Ken Paxton, the conservative Texas firebrand, could decide whether Republicans keep control of the Senate.
author: Michael Kruse and Lauren McGaughy

title: Colorado Supreme Court Orders Hospital to Restart Care for Transgender Minors
description: Children’s Hospital Colorado had halted hormone therapy and puberty blockers for transgender youths amid threats from the Trump administration to withhold funding from institutions that provide it.
author: Jack Healy

title: Channel Islands Fire Started Accidentally by Shipwrecked Sailor
description: The shipwrecked man was rescued from Channel Islands National Park, but the flares set off a 14,600-acre fire that has burned park buildings, forced evacuations and threatened rare Torrey pines.
author: Soumya Karlamangla

title: Trump Moves to Admit 10,000 More White South Africans as Refugees
description: The Trump administration argues that an “emergency refugee situation” in South Africa merits bringing more Afrikaners to America, at a cost of some $100 million.
author: Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Hamed Aleaziz
